steps.title: Enter two months of data
steps.content: At birth, $x=0$ and the whale is $157$ cm long. After $1$ month, it has grown by $4.6$ cm, so the length is $161.6$ cm. Use the points $(0,157)$ and $(1,161.6)$.

steps.title: Fit the linear model
steps.content: The slope is $$m=\frac{161.6-157}{1-0}=4.6.$$ The $y$-intercept is the starting length, $157$, so the equation is $y=4.6x+157$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $y=157x$ makes $157$ the monthly growth rate and starts at $0$. $y=157x+157$ has the right starting length but the wrong monthly growth. $y=4.6x+4.6$ has the right monthly growth but starts at $4.6$ cm. The model $y=4.6x+157$ has growth rate $4.6$ and starting length $157$, so D is correct.
